name: Internal Email with CEO Fraud Characteristics (Spoofed Sender)
description: Detects fraudulent emails with 'CEO Fraud' or internal spoofing characteristics. An email that appears to originate from a key executive in the organization is received by an internal user, but its origin ('OriginalClientAddress') does not correspond to a legitimate mail server, suggesting a compromised internal account or a phishing attempt.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the Exchange host **{computer_name}**: an email with characteristics of internal spoofing has been detected. The apparent sender is an executive (**{sender}**), but the source IP address (**{original_client_address}**) does not belong to any of our legitimate mail servers. This is a strong indicator of a potential CEO Fraud attack, a compromised account, or an internal phishing attempt (**MITRE ATT&CK T1566.004**). The message was sent to recipient **{receiver}** with the subject: **{subject}**. Immediate action is required to verify the sender's account, investigate the email's origin, and notify the user not to reply. This alert has a high priority level due to the severe potential impact of such attacks.

parameters.name: sender
parameters.description: The email address of the sender (e.g., ceo@yourcompany.com).

parameters.name: original_client_address
parameters.description: The source IP address of the client that sent the email.

parameters.name: receiver
parameters.description: The email address of the internal recipient.

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The name of the Exchange server where the event occurred.
